ground_truth.title: Top 10: Trends in Cloud Computing
ground_truth.author: James Darley
ground_truth.main_content: Today, cloud computing is a fast-paced sector guided by technological innovations and trends including quantum mechanics, sustainability and agentic AI. Recent projections by Gartner suggest that end-users will spend north of US$723bn on cloud technologies by the end of 2025. AI is rapidly reshaping cloud architecture, economics and security, becoming the sun around which all other trends pertaining to the cloud now orbit. The top 10 trends defining cloud computing in 2025 are: 1. Agentic AI - Advanced reasoning capabilities forming the brain for autonomous agents interacting with cloud services. 2. Hybrid and multi-cloud technologies - Strategic distribution of workloads across multiple public clouds and private infrastructure. 3. Cloud-native technologies - Leveraging containers, Kubernetes and microservices for scalable, resilient applications. 4. The expansion of intelligent edge - Extending cloud power closer to where data is generated for real-time processing. 5. Advanced cloud security - Proactive, data-driven strategies including Zero Trust Architecture powered by AI. 6. FinOps and cloud economics - Cultural practice of financial accountability for cloud spending. 7. Sustainable cloud computing - Powering operations with 100% renewable energy and carbon footprint tools. 8. The rise of low-code and no-code systems - Abstracting infrastructure complexity to empower citizen developers. 9. Industry-specific clouds - Curated, vertically-integrated solutions with pre-built compliance frameworks. 10. Quantum Computing as a Service - Cloud access to quantum hardware for complex optimization and simulation problems.
with: Today, cloud computing is a fast-paced sector guided by technological innovations and trends including quantum mechanics, sustainability and agentic AI.
AI is rapidly reshaping cloud architecture, economics and security, becoming the sun around which all other trends pertaining to the cloud now orbit.
Recent projections by Gartner suggest that end-users will spend north of US$723bn on cloud technologies by the end of 2025.
The era of allegiance to a single cloud vendor is decisively over. In 2025, hybrid and multi-cloud strategies are the norm.
The most transformative trend of 2025 is the leap from Gen AI as a passive assistant to Agentic AI as an active, autonomous participant in cloud operations.
without: Technology Magazine connects the leading technology executives of the world's largest brands.

items.caption: Technology Magazine looks into some of the latest trends that are driving the cloud computing sector
